::: {.chapter-num}
Chapter III
:::

## What hangs together {.chapter-title}

::: {.chapter-lede}
Symptoms rarely move alone.
Some travel as a chorus, others move opposite — and a few have nothing to say to each other at all.
The next two charts ask: which of your trackers tend to move in step?
:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### Correlation matrix

This is a grid where every tracker meets every other tracker.
The colour of each square answers the question: *when this one goes up, does that one tend to go up too, or down?*
**Warm tiles = move together** (both rise on the same days).
**Cool tiles = move opposite** (one rises while the other falls).
**Pale tiles = no clear relationship.**

The trackers are reordered so that things that behave alike sit near each other — this makes blocks of warm or cool colour easier to spot.
None of this proves cause-and-effect; it just tells you which of your measurements are telling overlapping stories.

```{webr}
#| label: fact-correlation
#| input: ["fileText"]

if (exists("visible_mat_clean") && ncol(visible_mat_clean) >= 3) {
  cm <- suppressWarnings(
    cor(visible_mat_clean, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  )
  cm[is.na(cm)] <- 0
  diag(cm) <- NA

  idx_pos <- which.max(cm)
  rc_pos <- arrayInd(idx_pos, dim(cm))
  pos_a <- rownames(cm)[rc_pos[1]]
  pos_b <- colnames(cm)[rc_pos[2]]
  pos_r <- cm[idx_pos]

  idx_neg <- which.min(cm)
  rc_neg <- arrayInd(idx_neg, dim(cm))
  neg_a <- rownames(cm)[rc_neg[1]]
  neg_b <- colnames(cm)[rc_neg[2]]
  neg_r <- cm[idx_neg]

  short <- function(x) sub("^.* — ", "", x)

  htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
    paste0(
      '<p class="section-fact">',
      'Trackers that most often rise and fall together: <strong>%s &harr; %s</strong> (strength %+0.2f, where +1 means perfectly in step). ',
      'Trackers that most often move in opposite directions: <strong>%s &harr; %s</strong> (%+0.2f, where -1 means perfectly opposed).',
      '</p>'
    ),
    short(pos_a), short(pos_b), pos_r,
    short(neg_a), short(neg_b), neg_r
  ))
}
```

```{webr}
#| label: chart-correlation
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 11

if (exists("visible_mat_clean") && ncol(visible_mat_clean) >= 3) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  cm <- suppressWarnings(
    cor(visible_mat_clean, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  )
  cm[is.na(cm)] <- 0

  d <- stats::as.dist(1 - abs(cm))
  ord <- stats::hclust(d, method = "average")$order
  cm_ord <- cm[ord, ord]

  cm_df <- data.frame(
    var1 = factor(rownames(cm_ord)[row(cm_ord)],
                  levels = rownames(cm_ord)),
    var2 = factor(colnames(cm_ord)[col(cm_ord)],
                  levels = rownames(cm_ord)),
    r = as.vector(cm_ord)
  )

  ggplot(cm_df, aes(var1, var2, fill = r)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = viz$secondary, mid = viz$paper_card,
                         high = viz$accent, midpoint = 0,
                         limits = c(-1, 1), name = "Move\ntogether →") +
    coord_equal() +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
         title = "Which trackers move together?",
         subtitle = "Warm = rise and fall in step · cool = move opposite · trackers grouped by similarity") +
    viz$theme(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1,
                                  size = 9),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
    )
}
```

:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### Symptom clusters

The same idea, drawn as a tree.
Trackers that join up early (towards the right edge of the chart) move very similarly — they tell overlapping stories.
Trackers that join far to the left are barely related.
If two trackers you'd expect to behave similarly fall into different branches, that's a small surprise worth a second look.

```{webr}
#| label: chart-clusters
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 9

if (exists("visible_mat_clean") && ncol(visible_mat_clean) >= 3) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  cm <- suppressWarnings(
    cor(visible_mat_clean, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  )
  cm[is.na(cm)] <- 0
  d <- stats::as.dist(1 - abs(cm))

  hc <- stats::hclust(d, method = "ward.D2")
  ddata <- ggdendro::dendro_data(hc, type = "rectangle")
  seg <- ggdendro::segment(ddata)
  lab <- ggdendro::label(ddata)
  lab$label <- sub("^.* — ", "", as.character(lab$label))

  ggplot() +
    geom_segment(data = seg,
                 aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),
                 colour = viz$ink_soft, linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_text(data = lab,
              aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
              hjust = 0, nudge_y = -0.02,
              colour = viz$ink_soft, size = 3.2) +
    coord_flip(clip = "off") +
    scale_y_reverse(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.45))) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(add = 0.6)) +
    labs(title = "Trackers that travel together",
         subtitle = "Branches joining further right = trackers that move more alike",
         x = NULL, y = NULL) +
    viz$theme(base_size = 11) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
          axis.text = element_blank(),
          axis.ticks = element_blank(),
          plot.margin = margin(15, 15, 15, 15),
          plot.title = element_text(family = "serif",
                                    face = "italic", size = 14,
                                    colour = viz$ink),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = viz$muted, size = 9))
}
```

:::
